title: Hyperbaric Oxygen Brain Injury Treatment Trial

skipped_criteria: Marshall CT score >1 required for GCS 7-8 or alcohol level >200 mg/dl: CT scoring and lab values are not available in the schema.
First hyperbaric oxygen treatment must be initiated within 8 hours of admission (non-surgical) or 14 hours (surgical): treatment initiation timing and surgical status are not in the schema.
First hyperbaric oxygen treatment cannot be initiated within 24 hours of injury: treatment initiation timing relative to injury is not in the schema.
GCS of 3 with mid-position and non-reactive pupils bilaterally (4mm): pupil reactivity and position are not available fields in the schema.
Penetrating head injury: gsw and stab are used as proxies in the exclusion rules but may not cover all penetrating head injury cases (e.g., impalement).
Pre-existing neurologic disease with confounding residual neurologic deficits: prior medical history fields are not in the schema.
Fixed coagulopathy: coagulation status is not in the schema.
Severe hypoxia: oxygenation/SpO2 values are not in the schema.
Cardiopulmonary resuscitation performed: cardiac arrest / CPR history is not an available field in the schema.
Coma suspected to be due to primarily non-TBI causes: clinical judgment criterion not expressible in the schema.
Any contraindications to the study intervention (hyperbaric oxygen): contraindication details are not in the schema.
Unstable acute spinal cord injury: spinal_injury_suspected captures suspicion but not instability or acuity; encoded as soft exclusion proxy only.
